topic.title: SAP TM Business Documents & Transportation Order Types

learningObjectives.title: Understand TOR document types
learningObjectives.description: Identify Transportation Order-based documents and their lifecycle role in SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Explain end-to-end document flow
learningObjectives.description: Describe progression from ERP trigger documents to freight settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate FU, FO and FB
learningObjectives.description: Clarify planning and execution differences among core TM documents.

sections.content: SAP TM manages transportation using Transportation Orders (TOR). Different TOR types represent different stages of the transportation lifecycle, from transportation demand to execution and settlement.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Transportation Order (TOR) is the parent business object in SAP TM. Freight Unit, Freight Order, Freight Booking and Forwarding Order are different TOR document types.

A Forwarding Order represents a customer's transportation request. It is commonly used by logistics service providers (LSPs) who receive transportation requests from customers and execute them through planning and carrier assignment.
Freight Units are automatically created from ERP documents such as Outbound Deliveries or Purchase Orders. They represent transportation demand and are the primary planning objects in SAP TM.
A Freight Order is generated after transportation planning. It contains routes, stages, assigned resources, carrier information and execution details required for transporting goods.
Freight Booking is primarily used for ocean and air transportation where transportation capacity must be reserved with external carriers before execution.

sections.title: Freight Unit vs Freight Order vs Freight Booking

sections.content: Three outbound deliveries are generated for a customer. SAP TM creates three Freight Units. The optimizer consolidates them into one Freight Order and assigns a carrier. The shipment is executed and transportation charges are settled using a Freight Settlement Document.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An FMCG company generates over 5,000 Freight Units every day. SAP TM automatically consolidates them into optimized Freight Orders, reducing the number of truck trips and lowering transportation costs.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: One of the most common SAP TM interview questions is 'Explain the complete transportation document lifecycle.' Practice explaining the document flow from Sales Order to Freight Settlement without hesitation.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Freight Booking with Freight Order. Freight Booking is mainly used for Air and Ocean transportation, whereas Freight Order is used for transportation execution across different modes.

items: Transportation Order is the parent business object.
Freight Booking is mainly used for air and ocean transport.
Freight Settlement Document settles transportation charges.
